opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: I can't even walk to the letterbox without gasping for air, and today I felt so dizzy I thought I was going to black out.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: Progressive breathlessness on minimal exertion and an episode of near-fainting while walking across the house today.

symptom_narrative.onset: progressive over 3 months, sharply worse over the last week
symptom_narrative.duration: 3 months of worsening exertional breathlessness, with today's exertional near-faint
symptom_narrative.character: breathlessness on minimal exertion, severe fatigue, and dizziness/near-fainting on walking
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: can't walk to the letterbox without gasping; nearly blacked out walking across the house today
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: breathing trouble in the chest; ankle swelling and abdominal fullness
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: brought on by exertion; today an episode of near-fainting while walking
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: any exertion (walking, exertion brings on the dizziness/breathlessness)
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: severe fatigue and lethargy, increasing ankle swelling and a feeling of abdominal fullness; denies chest pain, denies orthopnoea or waking gasping at night
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: gradually declining over months; today's near-faint on exertion is new
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: highly restricted, fearful of any exertion because of the breathlessness and dizziness

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: lives alone; high risk of a fall if she exerts and faints

psychosocial_profile.hidden_agenda: frightened by the near-faint and rapid decline; worried about coping alone

communication_style.typical_message_style: describes the progressive exertional decline clearly
